DELAYED MARINE REPORT. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR. 45 FOOT SAILBOAT SUSTAINED DAMAGE FROM STRONG THUNDERSTORM WINDS.

NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO. DAMAGE CONSISTED OF MAINLY UPROOTED HARDWOOD AND SOFTWOOD TREES...WITH SEVERAL RESIDENCES DAMAGED FROM FALLING TREES. MAXIMUM WINDS ARE ESTIMATED AT 100 MPH...WITH A TOTAL PATH LENGTH OF APPROXIMATELY 3.95 MILES. INITIAL TORNADO TOUCHDOWN OCCURRED 1 MILE NORTH OF THE NORTH TIP OF MANISTEE LAKE AT 147 PM...AND THE TORNADO LIFTED 2 MILES NORTH OF OXBOW LAKE AT APPROXIMATELY 158 PM.

NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO. DAMAGE CONSISTED OF MAINLY UPROOTED HARDWOOD AND SOFTWOOD TREES...WITH SEVERAL RESIDENCES DAMAGED FROM FALLING TREES. MAXIMUM WINDS ARE ESTIMATED AT 100 MPH...WITH A TOTAL PATH LENGTH OF APPROXIMATELY 3.95 MILES. INITIAL TORNADO TOUCHDOWN OCCURRED 1 MILE NORTH OF THE NORTH TIP OF MANISTEE LAKE AT 153 PM...AND THE TORNADO LIFTED 2 MILES NORTH OF OXBOW LAKE AT APPROXIMATELY 159 PM.

A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO. DAMAGE CONSISTED OF UPROOTED AND SNAPPED TREES AS WELL AS SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE TO ONE RESIDENCE AND SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO A BARN. THE MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 100 MPH. THE PATH OF LENGTH WAS 9.1 MILES WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 75 YARDS. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR APPROXIMATELY 13 MINUTES.
TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN. CALLER REPORTED SEEING FUNNEL CLOUD AS WELL.

A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO. DAMAGE CONSISTED OF UPROOTED HARDWOOD TREES AND SOFTWOOD TREES WITH A NUMBER OF THESE TREES FALLING ON AND DAMAGING RESIDENCES. THE MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 110 MPH. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 7.3 MILES WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 210 YARDS.

A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-0 TORNADO. DAMAGE CONSISTED OF UPROOTED AND SNAPPED TREES. THE MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 80 MPH. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 0.2 MILES WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 30 YARDS.
